sources.use: interaction identity and browser-observed event timing

finding: Repeated browser trials are comparable only when source commit, deployment identity, device/browser identity, ordered interaction sequence, API support, and both independent budget sets remain invariant. Per-trial capture digests detect retained-observation substitution, while a failed trial must remain visible rather than being averaged into success.
design_implication: Vercel Studio should wrap at least two production capture results in the target-browser packet before making a repeatability statement. Deployment authenticity and physical-device identity remain separate verification obligations.

verification.route: isolated Node.js test runner over byte-identical source later committed through GitHub
covered: failed repeated trial retained without success upgrade

uncertainty: No target-browser execution, deployment identity verification, CI run, or physical iPhone observation was performed in this slice.
claim_limit: The artifact validates declared packet coherence and capture-byte integrity only; it does not prove browser support, deployment authenticity, causality, audibility, smoothness, or device identity.
falsification_route: Run two or more trials against one independently verified deployment and exact commit. Reject repeatability if support, budgets, sequence, retained capture digest, or either independent verdict differs materially.
